title: Media Console - Board and Batten Collection
summary: A three-bay media console with 2x2 board-and-batten trim across the face, two center drawers flanked by a pair of single doors, all on four 2x2 legs.
description:
The carcass is a plain 1x12 box — two sides, a bottom, and a top — divided into three bays by two center partitions. **The 2x2 trim pieces run front and back across the whole face and sides, which is what gives this console its board-and-batten look** rather than a plain flat front; attach these after the box and legs are assembled, since they read as an applied layer over the carcass, not structural framing.

The center bay holds two stacked drawers running on simple wood guides — the drawer guides and the center drawer guide are what the drawer sides actually ride on, so get those level before building the drawers themselves. The two outer bays each get a single door, framed with 1x3 top/bottom rails and 1x2 side stiles around a 1/4" plywood back panel.

**Build all four legs and attach them before the board-and-batten trim** — the trim pieces run past the leg tops in places, and trying to add legs afterward means notching around trim that's already nailed on.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the carcass
steps.body: Assemble the sides, top, bottom, and two center partitions into a three-bay box with pocket holes and glue.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the four 2x2 legs to the underside of the carcass before adding any trim.

steps.title: Add the board-and-batten trim
steps.body: Nail the front/back trim, side trim, and top trim pieces across the face and sides of the carcass, working around the legs.

steps.title: Install the drawer guides
steps.body: Level and attach the drawer guides, the center drawer guide, and the bottom drawer guides inside the center bay.

steps.title: Build the drawers
steps.body: Build the two drawer boxes from the drawer fronts, backs, sides, and bottoms, and fit them to run on the guides.

steps.title: Build and hang the doors
steps.body: Frame each door from the top/bottom rails and side stiles around a plywood back panel, then hang in the two outer bays.

steps.title: Add hardware and finish
steps.body: Attach knobs or handles to the drawers and doors, then sand and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Board and batten media console with two drawers and two doors
